title: Introduction to SingleArmMRCT
text: Multi-regional clinical trials (MRCTs) increasingly used global drug development allow simultaneous regulatory submissions across multiple regions. key requirement regional approval — particularly Japan Japanese MHLW guidelines — demonstration regional consistency: evidence treatment effect observed specific region (e.g., Japan) consistent overall trial result. Two widely used consistency evaluation methods, originally proposed Japanese guidelines, : Method 1 (Effect Retention Approach): Evaluates whether Region 1 retains least fraction π\pi overall treatment effect. Method 2 (Simultaneous Positivity Approach): Evaluates whether regional estimates simultaneously show positive effect direction benefit. methods originally developed two-arm randomised controlled trials. However, single-arm trials now common oncology rare disease settings, historical control comparisons standard. SingleArmMRCT package extends Method 1 Method 2 single-arm setting, treatment effect defined relative pre-specified historical control value.

title: Introduction to SingleArmMRCT
text: Regional Consistency Probability (RCP) defined probability consistency criterion satisfied, evaluated assumed true parameter values trial design stage. trial design said adequate regional consistency RCP exceeds pre-specified target (commonly 0.80).

title: Introduction to SingleArmMRCT
text: Let θ\theta denote endpoint parameter given endpoint (e.g., mean, proportion, rate). Method 1 requires Region 1 retains least fraction π\pi overall treatment effect: RCP1=Pr[(θ̂1−θ0)≥π×(θ̂−θ0)] \text{RCP}_1 = \Pr\!\left[\,(\hat{\theta}_1 - \theta_0) \geq \pi \times (\hat{\theta} - \theta_0)\,\right] θ̂1\hat{\theta}_1 treatment effect estimate Region 1, θ̂\hat{\theta} overall pooled estimate, θ0\theta_0 null (historical control) value, π∈[0,1]\pi \[0, 1] pre-specified retention threshold (typically π=0.5\pi = 0.5). consistency condition can rewritten D≥0D \geq 0, : D=(1−πf1)(θ̂1−θ0)−π(1−f1)(θ̂−1−θ0) D = \bigl(1 - \pi f_1\bigr)\,(\hat{\theta}_1 - \theta_0)   - \pi(1 - f_1)\,(\hat{\theta}_{-1} - \theta_0) f1=N1/Nf_1 = N_1/N regional allocation fraction θ̂−1\hat{\theta}_{-1} pooled estimate regions 2,…,J2, \ldots, J combined. assumption homogeneous treatment effects across regions, DD follows normal distribution mean (1−π)δ(1-\pi)\delta variance depends endpoint type, yielding closed-form expression RCP1\text{RCP}_1, δ=θ−θ0\delta = \theta - \theta_0 treatment effect. endpoints smaller value indicates benefit (e.g., hazard ratio, rate ratio), inequality direction reversed. See endpoint-specific vignettes exact formulae.

title: Introduction to SingleArmMRCT
text: Method 2 requires JJ regional estimates simultaneously demonstrate positive effect. endpoints larger value indicates benefit (continuous, binary, milestone survival, RMST): RCP2=Pr[θ̂j>θ0 j=1,…,J] \text{RCP}_2 = \Pr\!\left[\,\hat{\theta}_j > \theta_0 \;\text{ } j = 1, \ldots, J\,\right] endpoints smaller value indicates benefit (hazard ratio, rate ratio): RCP2=Pr[θ̂j<θ0 j=1,…,J] \text{RCP}_2 = \Pr\!\left[\,\hat{\theta}_j < \theta_0 \;\text{ } j = 1, \ldots, J\,\right] regional estimators independent across regions, RCP2\text{RCP}_2 factorises : RCP2=∏j=1JPr[θ̂j shows benefit] \text{RCP}_2 = \prod_{j=1}^{J} \Pr\!\left[\,\hat{\theta}_j \text{ shows benefit}\,\right]

title: Introduction to SingleArmMRCT
text: package provides pair functions six endpoint types. calculation function supports two approaches: "formula": Closed-form semi-analytical solution based normal approximation. Computationally fast , binary count endpoints, exact. "simulation": Monte Carlo simulation. Serves independent numerical check formula results.

title: Non-Survival Endpoints: Continuous, Binary, and Count
text: Let μ̂j\hat{\mu}_j denote sample mean Region jj. assumption individual observations independently identically distributed N(μ,σ2)N(\mu, \sigma^2) within region, regional sample means : μ̂j∼N(μ,σ2Nj),j=1,…,J \hat{\mu}_j \sim N\!\left(\mu,\; \frac{\sigma^2}{N_j}\right), \qquad j = 1, \ldots, J independently across regions. treatment effect relative historical control mean μ0\mu_0 δ=μ−μ0>0\delta = \mu - \mu_0 > 0.

title: Non-Survival Endpoints: Continuous, Binary, and Count
text: Method 1 (Effect Retention): RCP1=Pr[(μ̂1−μ0)≥π(μ̂−μ0)] \text{RCP}_1 = \Pr\!\left[\,(\hat{\mu}_1 - \mu_0) \geq \pi\,(\hat{\mu} - \mu_0)\,\right] Defining D=(μ̂1−μ0)−π(μ̂−μ0)D = (\hat{\mu}_1 - \mu_0) - \pi(\hat{\mu} - \mu_0), condition D≥0D \geq 0 equivalent : D=(1−πf1)(μ̂1−μ0)−π(1−f1)(μ̂−1−μ0)≥0 D = (1 - \pi f_1)\,(\hat{\mu}_1 - \mu_0) - \pi(1 - f_1)\,(\hat{\mu}_{-1} - \mu_0) \geq 0 μ̂−1\hat{\mu}_{-1} sample mean pooled regions 2,…,J2, \ldots, J. homogeneity: E[D]=(1−π)δ,Var(D)=(1−πf1)2σ2N1+[π(1−f1)]2σ2N−N1 E[D] = (1 - \pi)\,\delta, \qquad \mathrm{Var}(D) = (1 - \pi f_1)^2\,\frac{\sigma^2}{N_1}                 + \bigl[\pi(1 - f_1)\bigr]^2\,\frac{\sigma^2}{N - N_1} Therefore: RCP1=Φ((1−π)δ(1−πf1)2σ2/N1+{π(1−f1)}2σ2/(N−N1)) \text{RCP}_1 = \Phi\!\left(\frac{(1 - \pi)\,\delta}   {\sqrt{(1 - \pi f_1)^2\,\sigma^2/N_1         + \{\pi(1 - f_1)\}^2\,\sigma^2/(N - N_1)}}\right) Method 2 (Simultaneous Positivity): RCP2=Pr[μ̂j>μ0 j]=∏j=1JΦ(δNjσ) \text{RCP}_2 = \Pr\!\left[\,\hat{\mu}_j > \mu_0 \;\text{ } j\,\right] = \prod_{j=1}^{J} \Phi\!\left(\frac{\delta\,\sqrt{N_j}}{\sigma}\right)

title: Non-Survival Endpoints: Continuous, Binary, and Count
text: Let YjY_j denote number responders Region jj. independent Bernoulli trials common response rate pp: Yj∼Binomial(Nj,p),j=1,…,J Y_j \sim \mathrm{Binomial}(N_j,\; p), \qquad j = 1, \ldots, J independently across regions. regional response rate estimator p̂j=Yj/Nj\hat{p}_j = Y_j / N_j, overall estimator p̂=∑jYj/N\hat{p} = \sum_j Y_j / N, treatment effect δ=p−p0>0\delta = p - p_0 > 0.

title: Non-Survival Endpoints: Continuous, Binary, and Count
text: Method 1 (Effect Retention) — Exact Enumeration: RCP1=Pr[(p̂1−p0)≥π(p̂−p0)] \text{RCP}_1 = \Pr\!\left[\,(\hat{p}_1 - p_0) \geq \pi\,(\hat{p} - p_0)\,\right] additivity independent binomials, Y−1=∑j≥2Yj∼Binomial(N−N1,p)Y_{-1} = \sum_{j \geq 2} Y_j \sim \mathrm{Binomial}(N - N_1,\; p). formula approach enumerates combinations (y1,y−1)∈{0,…,N1}×{0,…,N−N1}(y_1, y_{-1}) \\{0, \ldots, N_1\} \times \{0, \ldots, N - N_1\} sums joint probabilities satisfying consistency condition: RCP1=∑y1=0N1∑y−1=0N−N1b(y1;N1,p)b(y−1;N−N1,p)⋅𝟏[y1N1−p0≥π(y1+y−1N−p0)] \text{RCP}_1 = \sum_{y_1=0}^{N_1} \sum_{y_{-1}=0}^{N-N_1}   b(y_1;\,N_1,\,p)\;b(y_{-1};\,N{-}N_1,\,p)   \cdot \mathbf{1}\!\left[\frac{y_1}{N_1} - p_0 \geq \pi\!\left(\frac{y_1+y_{-1}}{N} - p_0\right)\right] b(y;n,p)=(ny)py(1−p)n−yb(y;\,n,\,p) = \binom{n}{y}p^y(1-p)^{n-y}. Method 2 (Simultaneous Positivity): condition p̂j>p0\hat{p}_j > p_0 equivalent Yj≥yj,minY_j \geq y_{j,\min} yj,min=⌊Njp0⌋+1y_{j,\min} = \lfloor N_j p_0 \rfloor + 1. Denoting FBin(n,p)(k)F_{\mathrm{Bin}(n,\,p)}(k) CDF binomial distribution parameters nn pp evaluated kk: RCP2=∏j=1J[1−FBin(Nj,p)(yj,min−1)] \text{RCP}_2 = \prod_{j=1}^{J} \left[1 - F_{\mathrm{Bin}(N_j,\,p)}(y_{j,\min} - 1)\right]

title: Non-Survival Endpoints: Continuous, Binary, and Count
text: Count data modelled negative binomial distribution. total event count Region jj : Yj∼NB(μ=Njλ,size=Njϕ),j=1,…,J Y_j \sim \mathrm{NB}\!\left(\mu = N_j\,\lambda,\;\; \mathrm{size} = N_j\,\phi\right), \qquad j = 1, \ldots, J independently across regions, λ>0\lambda > 0 expected count per patient alternative ϕ>0\phi > 0 dispersion parameter. regional rate estimator λ̂j=Yj/Nj\hat{\lambda}_j = Y_j / N_j, treatment effect expressed rate ratio: RR̂j=λ̂jλ0 \widehat{RR}_j = \frac{\hat{\lambda}_j}{\lambda_0} Benefit indicated RR=λ/λ0<1RR = \lambda / \lambda_0 < 1. reproducibility property negative binomial, pooled count regions 2,…,J2, \ldots, J follows NB(μ=(N−N1)λ,size=(N−N1)ϕ)\mathrm{NB}(\mu = (N - N_1)\lambda,\; \mathrm{size} = (N - N_1)\phi), enabling exact enumeration analogous binary case.

title: Non-Survival Endpoints: Continuous, Binary, and Count
text: Method 1 (log-RR scale): RCP1,log=Pr[log(RR̂1)≤πlog(RR̂)] \text{RCP}_{1,\log} = \Pr\!\left[\,\log(\widehat{RR}_1) \leq \pi\,\log(\widehat{RR})\,\right] Since RR<1RR < 1 (benefit), log(RR)<0\log(RR) < 0, condition requires log(RR̂1)\log(\widehat{RR}_1) sufficiently negative relative overall log(RR̂)\log(\widehat{RR}). Method 1 (linear-RR scale): RCP1,lin=Pr[(1−RR̂1)≥π(1−RR̂)] \text{RCP}_{1,\text{lin}} = \Pr\!\left[\,(1 - \widehat{RR}_1) \geq \pi\,(1 - \widehat{RR})\,\right] Method 1 variants use exact enumeration (y1,y−1)(y_1, y_{-1}) combinations via outer product negative binomial PMFs. Method 2: Denoting FNB(μ,ϕ)(k)F_{\mathrm{NB}(\mu,\,\phi)}(k) CDF negative binomial distribution mean μ\mu size ϕ\phi evaluated kk, condition RR̂j<1\widehat{RR}_j < 1 equivalent Yj<Njλ0Y_j < N_j\lambda_0, .e., Yj≤⌊Njλ0⌋−1Y_j \leq \lfloor N_j\lambda_0 \rfloor - 1 Njλ0N_j\lambda_0 integer (Yj≤Njλ0−1Y_j \leq N_j\lambda_0 - 1 otherwise). Therefore: RCP2=∏j=1JPr(RR̂j<1)=∏j=1JFNB(Njλ,Njϕ)(⌊Njλ0⌋−1) \text{RCP}_2 = \prod_{j=1}^{J} \Pr\!\left(\widehat{RR}_j < 1\right) = \prod_{j=1}^{J} F_{\mathrm{NB}(N_j\lambda,\,N_j\phi)}\!\left(\lfloor N_j\lambda_0 \rfloor - 1\right)

title: Non-Survival Endpoints: Continuous, Binary, and Count
text: Setting: λ=2\lambda = 2, λ0=3\lambda_0 = 3, ϕ=1\phi = 1, N=100N = 100 (J=3J = 3 regions N1=20N_1 = 20), π=0.5\pi = 0.5. output reports three RCP values: Method 1 log-RR scale (Method1_logRR), Method 1 linear-RR scale (Method1_linearRR), Method 2 (Method2).

title: Survival Endpoints: Hazard Ratio, Milestone Survival, and RMST
text: survival endpoints, following parameters define trial design. common parameters used throughout examples vignette.
code: lambda  <- log(2) / 10   # treatment arm: median survival = 10 lambda0 <- log(2) / 5    # historical control: median survival = 5 t_a     <- 3             # accrual period t_f     <- 10            # follow-up period # True HR = lambda / lambda0 = 0.5

title: Survival Endpoints: Hazard Ratio, Milestone Survival, and RMST
text: exponential model uniform accrual [0,ta][0, t_a] administrative censoring τ\tau, expected event probability per patient : ϕ=λλ+λd[1−e−(λ+λd)tf−e−(λ+λd)τ(λ+λd)ta] \phi = \frac{\lambda}{\lambda + \lambda_d} \left[1 - \frac{e^{-(\lambda + \lambda_d)t_f} - e^{-(\lambda + \lambda_d)\tau}}{(\lambda + \lambda_d)\,t_a}\right] expected number events Region jj Ej=NjϕE_j = N_j\,\phi, log-hazard ratio estimator approximate distribution: log(HR̂j)∼N(log(HR),1Ej) \log(\widehat{HR}_j) \sim N\!\left(\log(HR),\; \frac{1}{E_j}\right)

title: Survival Endpoints: Hazard Ratio, Milestone Survival, and RMST
text: Method 1 (log-HR scale): Letting δ=log(HR)<0\delta = \log(HR) < 0, E1=N1ϕE_1 = N_1\phi, E−1=(N−N1)ϕE_{-1} = (N - N_1)\phi: RCP1,log=Φ(−(1−π)δ(1−πf1)2/E1+{π(1−f1)}2/E−1) \text{RCP}_{1,\log} = \Phi\!\left(\frac{-(1 - \pi)\,\delta}   {\sqrt{(1 - \pi f_1)^2/E_1 + \{\pi(1 - f_1)\}^2/E_{-1}}}\right) Method 1 (linear-HR scale) (Hayashi Itoh 2018): RCP1,lin=Pr[(1−HR̂1)≥π(1−HR̂)] \text{RCP}_{1,\text{lin}} = \Pr\!\left[\,(1 - \widehat{HR}_1) \geq \pi\,(1 - \widehat{HR})\,\right] derived via delta method. Define g=log(HR̂1)−log(1−π+πHR̂)g = \log(\widehat{HR}_1) - \log(1 - \pi + \pi\,\widehat{HR}). homogeneity: E[g]=log(HR)−log(1−π+πHR) E[g] = \log(HR) - \log(1 - \pi + \pi\,HR) Var(g)=1Etotal[{1−π+π(1−f1)HR}2f1(1−π+πHR)2+{π(1−f1)HR}2(1−f1)(1−π+πHR)2] \mathrm{Var}(g) = \frac{1}{E_{\text{total}}} \left[   \frac{\{1 - \pi + \pi(1 - f_1)HR\}^2}{f_1\,(1 - \pi + \pi\,HR)^2}   + \frac{\{\pi(1 - f_1)HR\}^2}{(1 - f_1)\,(1 - \pi + \pi\,HR)^2} \right] RCP1,lin=Φ(−E[g]/Var(g))\text{RCP}_{1,\text{lin}} = \Phi(-E[g] / \sqrt{\mathrm{Var}(g)}). Method 2: RCP2=∏j=1JPr(HR̂j<1)=∏j=1JΦ(−δEj) \text{RCP}_2 = \prod_{j=1}^{J} \Pr\!\left(\widehat{HR}_j < 1\right) = \prod_{j=1}^{J} \Phi\!\left(-\delta\,\sqrt{E_j}\right)

title: Survival Endpoints: Hazard Ratio, Milestone Survival, and RMST
code: result_f <- rcp1armHazardRatio(   lambda         = lambda,   lambda0        = lambda0,   Nj             = c(20, 80),   t_a            = t_a,   t_f            = t_f,   lambda_dropout = NULL,   PI             = 0.5,   approach       = "formula" ) print(result_f) #>  #> Regional Consistency Probability for Single-Arm MRCT #> Endpoint : Time-to-Event (Hazard Ratio) #>  #>    Approach       : title: Survival Endpoints: Hazard Ratio, Milestone Survival, and RMST
text: treatment effect evaluation time tevalt_{\text{eval}} : δ=S(teval)−S0=e−λteval−S0 \delta = S(t_{\text{eval}}) - S_0 = e^{-\lambda\,t_{\text{eval}}} - S_0 S0S_0 historical control survival rate tevalt_{\text{eval}}, supplied user. asymptotic variance Kaplan-Meier estimator Ŝj(teval)\hat{S}_j(t_{\text{eval}}) derived Greenwood’s formula: Var[Ŝj(t)]≈e−2λtNj∫0tλe(λ+λd)uGa(u)du=:vKM(t)Nj \mathrm{Var}\!\left[\hat{S}_j(t)\right] \approx \frac{e^{-2\lambda t}}{N_j} \int_0^{t} \frac{\lambda\,e^{(\lambda + \lambda_d)u}}{G_a(u)}\,du \;=:\; \frac{v_{KM}(t)}{N_j} : Ga(u)={10≤u≤tf(τ−u)/tatf<u≤τ G_a(u) = \begin{cases} 1 & 0 \leq u \leq t_f \\ (\tau - u)/t_a & t_f < u \leq \tau \end{cases} Closed-form solution teval≤tft_{\text{eval}} \leq t_f (Ga(u)=1G_a(u) = 1 throughout): vKM(t)=e−2λt⋅λλ+λd(e(λ+λd)t−1) v_{KM}(t) = e^{-2\lambda t} \cdot \frac{\lambda}{\lambda + \lambda_d} \left(e^{(\lambda + \lambda_d)\,t} - 1\right) λd=0\lambda_d = 0 reduces binomial variance S(t)(1−S(t))S(t)(1 - S(t)). teval>tft_{\text{eval}} > t_f, integral evaluated numerically via stats::integrate().

text: Method 1: RCP1=Φ((1−π)δ(1−πf1)2vKM/N1+{π(1−f1)}2vKM/(N−N1)) \text{RCP}_1 = \Phi\!\left(\frac{(1 - \pi)\,\delta}   {\sqrt{(1 - \pi f_1)^2\,v_{KM}/N_1 + \{\pi(1 - f_1)\}^2\,v_{KM}/(N - N_1)}}\right) Method 2: RCP2=∏j=1JΦ(δvKM/Nj) \text{RCP}_2 = \prod_{j=1}^{J} \Phi\!\left(\frac{\delta}{\sqrt{v_{KM}/N_j}}\right)

title: Survival Endpoints: Hazard Ratio, Milestone Survival, and RMST
text: restricted mean survival time (RMST) truncation time τ*\tau^* : μ(τ*)=∫0τ*S(t)dt=1−e−λτ*λ \mu(\tau^*) = \int_0^{\tau^*} S(t)\,dt = \frac{1 - e^{-\lambda\tau^*}}{\lambda} asymptotic variance regional RMST estimator : Var[μ̂j(τ*)]≈vRMST(τ*)Nj \mathrm{Var}\!\left[\hat{\mu}_j(\tau^*)\right] \approx \frac{v_{\text{RMST}}(\tau^*)}{N_j} : vRMST(τ*)=∫0τ*eλdt(1−e−λ(τ*−t))2λGa(t)dt v_{\text{RMST}}(\tau^*) = \int_0^{\tau^*} \frac{e^{\lambda_d t}\left(1 - e^{-\lambda(\tau^* - t)}\right)^2}{\lambda\,G_a(t)}\,dt Closed-form solution τ*≤tf\tau^* \leq t_f, using (r)=(erτ*−1)/rA(r) = (e^{r\tau^*} - 1)/r ((0)=τ*(0) = \tau^*): $$ v_{\text{RMST}}(\tau^*) = \frac{1}{\lambda}\Bigl[   (\lambda_d)   - 2\,e^{-\lambda\tau^*}\,(\lambda_d + \lambda)   + e^{-2\lambda\tau^*}\,(\lambda_d + 2\lambda) \Bigr] $$ λd=0\lambda_d = 0 simplifies : vRMST(τ*)=τ*−2(1−e−λτ*)λ+1−e−2λτ*2λ v_{\text{RMST}}(\tau^*) = \tau^*   - \frac{2(1 - e^{-\lambda\tau^*})}{\lambda}   + \frac{1 - e^{-2\lambda\tau^*}}{2\lambda} τ*>tf\tau^* > t_f, integral split tft_f evaluated via stats::integrate().

title: Survival Endpoints: Hazard Ratio, Milestone Survival, and RMST
text: Method 1: RCP1=Φ((1−π)(μest−μ0)(1−πf1)2v/N1+{π(1−f1)}2v/(N−N1)) \text{RCP}_1 = \Phi\!\left(\frac{(1 - \pi)\,(\mu_{\text{est}} - \mu_0)}   {\sqrt{(1 - \pi f_1)^2\,v/N_1 + \{\pi(1 - f_1)\}^2\,v/(N - N_1)}}\right) Method 2: RCP2=∏j=1JΦ(μest−μ0v/Nj) \text{RCP}_2 = \prod_{j=1}^{J} \Phi\!\left(\frac{\mu_{\text{est}} - \mu_0}{\sqrt{v/N_j}}\right)

title: SingleArmMRCT
text: SingleArmMRCT provides functions calculate visualise Regional Consistency Probability (RCP) single-arm multi-regional clinical trials (MRCTs) using Effect Retention Approach (ERA). package addresses critical methodological gap: current Japanese MHLW Method 1 Method 2 consistency criteria originally developed two-arm trials, yet single-arm trials increasingly form basis regulatory submissions, particularly oncology. package extends classical approaches single-arm setting across six endpoint types.

title: SingleArmMRCT
text: Method 1 (Effect Retention): Evaluates whether Region 1 retains least fraction pi overall treatment effect. Method 2 (Simultaneous Positivity): Evaluates whether regional estimates exceed null value simultaneously.

title: SingleArmMRCT
text: function supports two approaches: "formula": Closed-form semi-analytical solution based normal approximation. "simulation": Monte Carlo simulation.

title: SingleArmMRCT
text: package yet CRAN. Install source:
code: # Install devtools if not already installed install.packages("devtools")  # Install SingleArmMRCT from local source devtools::install_local("path/to/SingleArmMRCT")

what: Regional Consistency Probability for Single-Arm MRCT (Binary Endpoint) — rcp1armBinary
title: Regional Consistency Probability for Single-Arm MRCT (Binary Endpoint) — rcp1armBinary
text: Calculate regional consistency probability (RCP) binary endpoints single-arm multi-regional clinical trials (MRCTs) using Effect Retention Approach (ERA). Two evaluation methods supported: Method 1: Effect retention approach. Evaluates whether Region 1 retains least fraction PI overall treatment effect: \(Pr[(\hat{p}_1 - p_0) > \pi \times (\hat{p} - p_0)]\). Method 2: Simultaneous positivity across regions. Evaluates whether regional response rates exceed null value: \(Pr[\hat{p}_j > p_0 \text{ } j]\). Two calculation approaches available: "formula": Exact closed-form solution via full enumeration binomial joint distribution. Method 1 uses two-block decomposition (Region 1 vs regions 2..J combined), valid \(J \geq 2\). Method 2 supports \(J \geq 2\) regions. "simulation": Monte Carlo simulation. Supports \(J \geq 2\) regions.

what: Regional Consistency Probability for Single-Arm MRCT (Count Endpoint) — rcp1armCount
title: Regional Consistency Probability for Single-Arm MRCT (Count Endpoint) — rcp1armCount
text: Calculate regional consistency probability (RCP) count (overdispersed) endpoints single-arm multi-regional clinical trials (MRCTs) using Effect Retention Approach (ERA). Count data modelled negative binomial distribution, treatment effect expressed rate ratio (RR) relative historical control rate \(\lambda_0\). Two effect scales considered: Log-RR scale: \(\log(\widehat{RR}_j) = \log(\hat{\lambda}_j / \lambda_0)\). Linear-RR scale: \(1 - \widehat{RR}_j = 1 - \hat{\lambda}_j / \lambda_0\). Two evaluation methods supported (scale): Method 1: Effect retention approach. Evaluates whether Region 1 retains least fraction PI overall treatment effect. Log-RR: \(\log(\widehat{RR}_1) < \pi \times \log(\widehat{RR})\); Linear-RR: \((1 - \widehat{RR}_1) > \pi \times (1 - \widehat{RR})\). Method 2: Simultaneous benefit across regions. Evaluates whether regional rate ratios 1: \(\widehat{RR}_j < 1\) \(j\). (Equivalent log-RR linear-RR scales.) Two calculation approaches available: "formula": Exact closed-form solution via full enumeration negative binomial joint distribution. Method 1 uses two-block decomposition (Region 1 vs regions 2..J combined), valid \(J \geq 2\). Method 2 supports \(J \geq 2\) regions. "simulation": Monte Carlo simulation. Supports \(J \geq 2\) regions.

what: Regional Consistency Probability for Single-Arm MRCT (Time-to-Event Endpoint) — rcp1armHazardRatio
title: Regional Consistency Probability for Single-Arm MRCT (Time-to-Event Endpoint) — rcp1armHazardRatio
text: Calculate regional consistency probability (RCP) time--event endpoints using hazard ratio (HR) single-arm multi-regional clinical trials (MRCTs) using Effect Retention Approach (ERA). Event times modelled exponential distribution hazard rate \(\lambda\) (treatment) relative known historical control hazard \(\lambda_0\). treatment effect expressed hazard ratio: \(HR = \lambda / \lambda_0 < 1\) (benefit). Two effect scales considered: Log-HR scale: \(\log(\widehat{HR}_j) = \log(\hat{\lambda}_j / \lambda_0)\). Linear-HR scale: \(1 - \widehat{HR}_j = 1 - \hat{\lambda}_j / \lambda_0\). Two evaluation methods supported (scale): Method 1: Effect retention approach. Evaluates whether Region 1 retains least fraction PI overall treatment effect. Log-HR: \(\log(\widehat{HR}_1) < \pi \times \log(\widehat{HR})\); Linear-HR: \((1 - \widehat{HR}_1) > \pi \times (1 - \widehat{HR})\). Method 2: Simultaneous benefit across regions. Evaluates whether regional hazard ratios 1: \(\widehat{HR}_j < 1\) \(j\). (Equivalent log-HR linear-HR scales.) Two calculation approaches available: "formula": Closed-form solution based normal approximation \(\log(\widehat{HR})\) delta method linear-HR scale (Hayashi Itoh 2018). Method 1 uses two-block decomposition (Region 1 vs regions 2..J combined), valid \(J \geq 2\). Method 2 supports \(J \geq 2\) regions. title: Regional Consistency Probability for Single-Arm MRCT (Milestone Survival Endpoint) — rcp1armMilestoneSurvival
text: Calculate regional consistency probability (RCP) milestone survival endpoints single-arm multi-regional clinical trials (MRCTs) using Effect Retention Approach (ERA). Event times modelled exponential distribution hazard rate \(\lambda\) (treatment). treatment effect prespecified evaluation time \(t_{\mathrm{eval}}\) expressed difference survival rates: \(\delta = S(t_{\mathrm{eval}}) - S_0(t_{\mathrm{eval}})\), \(S_0\) historical control survival rate \(t_{\mathrm{eval}}\). Two evaluation methods supported: Method 1: Effect retention approach. Evaluates whether Region 1 retains least fraction PI overall treatment effect: \(Pr[(\hat{S}_1(t) - S_0(t)) > \pi \times (\hat{S}(t) - S_0(t))]\). Method 2: Simultaneous benefit across regions. Evaluates whether regional Kaplan-Meier estimates exceed historical control: \(Pr[\hat{S}_j(t) > S_0(t) \text{ } j]\). Two calculation approaches available: "formula": Closed-form semi-analytical solution based asymptotic variance Kaplan-Meier estimator derived Greenwood's formula. \(t_{\mathrm{eval}} \leq t_f\), administrative censoring survival function \(G_a(t) = 1\) variance integral closed-form solution. \(t_{\mathrm{eval}} > t_f\), integral evaluated numerically via integrate. Method 1 uses two-block decomposition (Region 1 vs regions 2..J combined). Method 2 supports \(J \geq 2\) regions. "simulation": Monte Carlo simulation vectorized Kaplan-Meier estimation. Supports \(J \geq 2\) regions.

title: Regional Consistency Probability for Single-Arm MRCT (Milestone Survival Endpoint) — rcp1armMilestoneSurvival
text: Variance formula. asymptotic variance Kaplan-Meier estimator \(\hat{S}_j(t)\) single arm \(N_j\) subjects derived Greenwood's formula: $$   \mathrm{Var}[\hat{S}_j(t)] \approx   \frac{S^2(t)}{N_j} \int_0^t \frac{\lambda(u)}{S(u) \cdot G(u)} \, du, $$ \(G(u) = e^{-\lambda_d u} \cdot G_a(u)\) overall censoring survival function, $$   G_a(u) = \begin{cases}     1              & 0 \leq u \leq t_f, \\     (\tau - u)/t_a & t_f < u \leq \tau.   \end{cases} $$ exponential model \(S(u) = e^{-\lambda u}\), simplifies : $$   \mathrm{Var}[\hat{S}_j(t)] \approx   \frac{e^{-2\lambda t}}{N_j} \int_0^t   \frac{\lambda \, e^{(\lambda + \lambda_d) u}}{G_a(u)} \, du. $$ Closed-form solution (\(t_{\mathrm{eval}} \leq t_f\)). \(t_{\mathrm{eval}} \leq t_f\), \(G_a(u) = 1\) throughout \([0, t_{\mathrm{eval}}]\), integral reduces : $$   \int_0^t \lambda \, e^{(\lambda + \lambda_d) u} \, du   = \frac{\lambda}{\lambda + \lambda_d}     \bigl( e^{(\lambda + \lambda_d) t} - 1 \bigr). $$ Therefore: $$   \mathrm{Var}[\hat{S}_j(t)] =   \frac{e^{-2\lambda t}}{N_j} \cdot   \frac{\lambda}{\lambda + \lambda_d}   \bigl( e^{(\lambda + \lambda_d) t} - 1 \bigr). $$ dropout (\(\lambda_d = 0\)), simplifies binomial variance: $$   \mathrm{Var}[\hat{S}_j(t)] = \frac{S(t)(1 - S(t))}{N_j}. $$

title: Regional Consistency Probability for Single-Arm MRCT (RMST Endpoint) — rcp1armRMST
text: Calculate regional consistency probability (RCP) restricted mean survival time (RMST) endpoints single-arm multi-regional clinical trials (MRCTs) using Effect Retention Approach (ERA). Event times modelled exponential distribution hazard rate \(\lambda\) (treatment). treatment effect expressed difference RMST prespecified truncation time \(\tau^*\): \(\delta = \mu(\tau^*) - \mu_0(\tau^*)\), \(\mu_0(\tau^*)\) historical control RMST \(\tau^*\). regional RMST estimator defined area Kaplan-Meier curve \(\tau^*\): \(\hat{\mu}_j(\tau^*) = \int_0^{\tau^*} \hat{S}_j(t)\,dt\). Two evaluation methods supported: Method 1: Effect retention approach. Evaluates whether Region 1 retains least fraction PI overall treatment effect: \(Pr[(\hat{\mu}_1 - \mu_0) > \pi \times (\hat{\mu} - \mu_0)]\). Method 2: Simultaneous benefit across regions. Evaluates whether regional RMST estimates exceed historical control RMST: \(Pr[\hat{\mu}_j > \mu_0 \text{ } j]\). Two calculation approaches available: "formula": Normal approximation based exponential event times exponential dropout. \(\tau^* \leq t_f\) (common setting practice), administrative censoring survival function equals 1 \([0, \tau^*]\) variance integral closed-form solution. \(\tau^* > t_f\), integral evaluated numerically via integrate. Method 1 uses two-block decomposition (Region 1 vs regions 2..J combined), valid \(J \geq 2\). Method 2 supports \(J \geq 2\) regions. "simulation": Monte Carlo simulation. RMST simulation replicate computed exact area Kaplan-Meier step function (sum rectangles). Supports \(J \geq 2\) regions.

title: Regional Consistency Probability for Single-Arm MRCT (RMST Endpoint) — rcp1armRMST
text: Variance formula. asymptotic variance RMST estimator \(\hat{\mu}_j(\tau^*)\) single arm \(n\) subjects : $$   \mathrm{Var}(\hat{\mu}_j(\tau^*)) \approx   \frac{1}{n} \int_0^{\tau^*}   \frac{\left(e^{-\lambda t} - e^{-\lambda \tau^*}\right)^2}        {\lambda \cdot e^{-\lambda t} \cdot G(t)}   \,dt, $$ \(G(t) = P(\mathrm{observed\ time} \geq t)\) overall censoring survival function: $$   G(t) = e^{-\lambda_d t} \times G_a(t), \quad   G_a(t) = \begin{cases}     1              & 0 \leq t \leq t_f, \\     (\tau-t)/t_a   & t_f < t \leq \tau.   \end{cases} $$ Closed-form variance (\(\tau^* \leq t_f\)). \(\tau^* \leq t_f\), \(G_a(t) = 1\) \([0, \tau^*]\) integrand reduces \(e^{\lambda_d t}(1 - e^{-\lambda(\tau^*-t)})^2 / \lambda\). Expanding integrating term term: $$   v(\tau^*) = \frac{1}{\lambda}\Bigl[     (\lambda_d)     - 2 e^{-\lambda\tau^*} (\lambda_d + \lambda)     + e^{-2\lambda\tau^*} (\lambda_d + 2\lambda)   \Bigr], $$ \((r) = (e^{r\tau^*} - 1)/r\) \(r > 0\) \((0) = \tau^*\). dropout (\(\lambda_d = 0\)) reduces : $$   v(\tau^*) = \tau^* - \frac{2(1-e^{-\lambda\tau^*})}{\lambda}               + \frac{1-e^{-2\lambda\tau^*}}{2\lambda}. $$

text: Calculation functions — supports approach = "formula" (closed-form semi-analytical) approach = "simulation" (Monte Carlo), returns Method 1 Method 2 RCP: rcp1armContinuous(): Continuous endpoint. Method 1 uses normal approximation via two-block decomposition; Method 2 uses product independent regional probabilities. rcp1armBinary(): Binary endpoint. Method 1 uses exact enumeration binomial joint distribution; Method 2 uses product independent binomial tail probabilities. rcp1armCount(): Count endpoint (negative binomial model). Provides RCP log-RR scale linear-RR scale via reproducibility property negative binomial distribution. rcp1armHazardRatio(): Time--event endpoint (hazard ratio). Closed-form solution based normal approximation log(HR) delta method linear-HR scale (Hayashi Itoh 2018). Supports accrual period, follow-period, exponential dropout. rcp1armMilestoneSurvival(): Milestone survival endpoint. Variance Kaplan-Meier estimator derived Greenwood’s formula (Tang 2022). Returns "closed-form" t_eval <= t_f "numerical-integration" otherwise. rcp1armRMST(): Restricted mean survival time (RMST) endpoint. Variance formula via integration squared survival difference. Returns "closed-form" tau_star <= t_f "numerical-integration" otherwise.
